title: Canon Foundation Research Fellowships 2026 in Japan | Up to €30,000 Funding for European Researchers
summary: Applications are now open for the prestigious Canon Foundation Research Fellowships 2026, offering outstanding European researchers the opportunity to conduct fully funded international research in Japan for periods ranging from three months to one year. The Canon Foundation in Europe annually awards up to 15 fellowships to highly qualified researchers seeking to strengthen international collaboration, advance innovative research, and gain valuable global academic and professional experience. One of the biggest strengths of this programme is its flexibility. Fellows are free to choose their own host institutions in Japan and may conduct research in any academic or professional field without thematic restrictions. With funding support of up to €30,000 per year, the Canon Foundation Fellowship is considered one of the leading international research mobility programmes supporting European researchers in Japan. About the Canon Foundation Research Fellowships The Canon Foundation in Europe established this fellowship programme to encourage scientific, academic, and professional exchange between Europe and Japan. The programme supports international collaboration across all disciplines while promoting cross-cultural understanding and long-term research partnerships. Each year, selected fellows receive support to pursue independent research projects at Japanese universities, laboratories, research centres, companies, government institutions, or professional organisations. Unlike many fellowship programmes, applicants are not required to be currently employed or enrolled at a university at the time of application. This makes the opportunity highly accessible to independent researchers and professionals outside traditional academic settings. The fellowship supports all research areas, including but not limited to: Engineering and Technology Artificial Intelligence and Data Science Medical and Health Sciences Climate and Environmental Studies Economics and Business Social Sciences Humanities Education Public Policy Architecture and Urban Development Agriculture and Food Sciences Physics and Chemistry Arts and Cultural Studies Fellowship Duration The Canon Foundation Research Fellowship offers flexible research durations: Minimum duration: 3 months Maximum duration: 12 months Research projects may begin anytime between January 2027 and December 2027. Fellowship activities may continue into 2028 depending on the approved project schedule. Financial Benefits The Canon Foundation provides generous funding support of up to €30,000 per year. Shorter fellowship periods receive pro-rated funding. The fellowship grant can be used for: Living expenses International travel costs Accommodation Insurance Research materials and supplies Laboratory or fieldwork expenses Books and academic resources Other professional and research-related costs Importantly, the Foundation does not impose strict spending restrictions, giving fellows flexibility in managing their research budgets. Eligibility Criteria The Canon Foundation Research Fellowship is open to European researchers wishing to conduct research in Japan. Eligible regions include: European Union countries United Kingdom Switzerland Israel Turkey Balkan countries Baltic countries Applicants should hold at least: A Master’s degree, or A PhD degree The qualification should typically have been obtained within the last ten years before applying. However, applicants who completed their qualifications more than ten years ago may still be considered if they provide additional supporting information explaining their circumstances. Key Features of the Fellowship 1. No Restrictions on Research Fields Researchers from all academic and professional disciplines are eligible to apply. 2. Freedom to Choose Host Institution Applicants may independently select their preferred host institution in Japan, including universities, laboratories, companies, government institutions, and research organisations. The Foundation does not assign placements or provide host matching services, so applicants must arrange their host institution themselves. 3. Open Beyond Academia The fellowship welcomes applications from professionals working in: Academia Industry Government institutions Commercial organisations Professional organisations 4. No Current Employment Requirement Applicants do not need to be enrolled at a university or employed by an organisation when applying. 5. Priority for First-Time Research Experience in Japan Preference is generally given to applicants travelling to Japan for the first time rather than those extending an existing stay abroad. Required Application Documents Applicants must upload the following documents through the online application portal: Mandatory Documents Research Plan (maximum 2 A4 pages) Research objectives Methodology Expected outcomes Estimated total costs Curriculum Vitae (CV) Highest Academic Degree Certificate Passport-size Photograph Publications or Professional Achievements List Chronological format Include all authors Publication year Page references Two Reference Letters From academic or professional referees familiar with the applicant’s qualifications and work Host Institution Confirmation Letter Confirmation of support Availability of facilities Description of supervision arrangements Important Information About Missing Documents Applicants whose reference letters or host institution confirmation letters are not ready by the deadline should still submit their application before the official closing date. Missing documents may be uploaded until: 30 September 2026, 23:59 CET Alternatively, referees and host supervisors may directly email their letters to: canon.foundation@canon-europe.com Applicants must remember to re-submit their application after uploading additional documents. Application Deadline The application deadline for the Canon Foundation Research Fellowships 2026 is: 15 September 2026, 23:59 CET Selection Process and Success Rate The Canon Foundation Fellowship is highly competitive, with an average success rate of approximately 10%. Applications are evaluated based on: Quality of the research proposal Strength of recommendation letters Feasibility of the research project Support from the host institution The Foundation states that funding decisions are not influenced by: Gender Age Nationality Successful applicants will be notified by mid-December 2026. Important Conditions Applicants should carefully note the following conditions: The Foundation does not assist applicants in finding host institutions Applicants holding concurrent research grants from other organisations are generally not eligible Priority is given to applicants beginning new international research stays Applications must be submitted through the official online portal Why You Should Apply The Canon Foundation Research Fellowship provides an exceptional opportunity to conduct fully funded international research while building meaningful collaborations between Europe and Japan. Selected fellows benefit from: Significant financial support International research exposure Flexible research arrangements Freedom to choose host institutions Professional networking opportunities Enhanced global career prospects Cross-cultural collaboration and learning Researchers seeking international academic and professional experience in Japan should strongly consider applying for this prestigious fellowship programme. How to Apply Interested applicants can apply through the official application portal HERE . Click HERE for more information about the Canon Foundation Research Fellowships 2026 in Japan. Explore more global job opportunities on OFY here . The post Canon Foundation Research Fellowships 2026 in Japan | Up to €30,000 Funding for European Researchers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: UNFPA Young Creators Lab 2026: Fully Online Regional Programme for Young Digital Creators Across Asia and the Pacific
summary: Applications are now officially open for the 2026 UNFPA Young Creators Lab, an exciting virtual regional programme designed to empower young creators and youth-led organizations across Asia and the Pacific with the skills needed to produce reliable, ethical, inclusive, and rights-based digital content. Organized by United Nations Population Fund (UNFPA), the Young Creators Lab aims to equip the next generation of digital storytellers, advocates, influencers, educators, and youth leaders with practical tools to tackle misinformation and create impactful online content around comprehensive sexuality education, gender equality, sexual and reproductive health and rights (SRHR), online safety, healthy relationships, and youth empowerment. As young people increasingly rely on social media platforms, short-form videos, and digital creators for information about health, relationships, gender, and rights, the need for accurate and responsible online content has become more important than ever. The UNFPA Young Creators Lab responds to this urgent need by building the capacity of young creators to lead positive digital change across the region. The programme will run fully online from August to October 2026 and will feature nine live interactive sessions delivered every Tuesday at 11:00 AM Bangkok time. The application deadline is 17 July 2026. About the UNFPA Young Creators Lab The UNFPA Young Creators Lab is a regional virtual learning and mentorship programme focused on helping young creators produce trustworthy and engaging digital content for young audiences. The programme recognizes that digital platforms are now one of the most influential spaces where young people learn about: Their bodies and health Relationships and consent Gender equality Sexual and reproductive health Rights and inclusion Mental wellbeing Identity and self-expression However, alongside the opportunities created by digital media, online spaces also contain misinformation, harmful stereotypes, deepfakes, technology-facilitated gender-based violence, unsafe online practices, and misleading health content. To address these challenges, UNFPA has created this innovative learning platform to help young creators become responsible digital educators and advocates. Programme Duration and Format The 2026 Young Creators Lab will run from August to October 2026 as a fully online programme. Participants will attend: Nine live virtual learning sessions Interactive quizzes after every session Sprint activities and collaborative learning exercises Mentorship opportunities Community engagement activities Optional capstone project development All sessions will take place on Tuesdays at 11:00 AM Bangkok time. What Participants Will Learn The programme is structured around eight major learning pillars that focus on modern digital communication, ethical content creation, online safety, advocacy, and audience engagement. 1. Digital Comprehensive Sexuality Education Content Production Participants will learn how to create engaging, accurate, youth-friendly digital content focused on comprehensive sexuality education and life skills. 2. Messaging for Gender Equality and Positive Masculinity The Lab will help creators understand how to produce content that promotes healthy masculinity, respectful relationships, inclusion, and gender equality. 3. AI Fluency for Creators Young creators will gain practical exposure to artificial intelligence tools and learn how AI can support responsible content creation, digital storytelling, and communication. 4. Critical Assessment of Misinformation Participants will learn how to identify misinformation, verify facts, evaluate online claims, and counter harmful narratives related to health and rights. 5. Technology-Facilitated Gender-Based Violence Prevention and Safety by Design The programme will teach online safety principles and strategies for protecting creators and audiences from digital abuse, harassment, and harmful online practices. 6. Universal Design Principles for the Digital Era Participants will explore inclusive and accessible content design practices to ensure digital materials can reach broader and more diverse audiences. 7. Audience Growth and the Algorithm Creators will learn strategies for audience engagement, platform growth, visibility, and understanding how social media algorithms impact content performance. 8. Online Voice, Advocacy and Amplification The programme will support participants in strengthening their digital advocacy skills, public voice, online campaigns, and community impact. Capstone Project Opportunity In addition to the training sessions, participants can choose to join an optional capstone project track. Under mentor guidance, participants may develop: A comprehensive sexuality education campaign Digital educational content Advocacy campaigns Multi-creator collaborations Amplification toolkits Social media storytelling initiatives Selected capstone projects will be showcased during the programme’s Premiere Day event. Benefits of the UNFPA Young Creators Lab Participants selected for the programme will receive numerous professional and educational benefits, including: Practical Digital Media Skills Participants will gain hands-on skills in content production, advocacy, digital storytelling, AI literacy, and audience engagement. Regional Networking Opportunities Creators from different countries across Asia and the Pacific will connect, collaborate, and exchange ideas. Mentorship and Guidance Participants will receive mentorship support while developing projects and strengthening their online impact. Exposure to International Learning The programme provides direct learning opportunities connected to a United Nations agency working globally on youth empowerment and reproductive health rights. UNFPA Completion Certificate Participants who successfully complete the programme requirements will receive the UNFPA Young Creators Lab Completion Certificate. Opportunity to Showcase Projects Capstone projects may be featured during Premiere Day, providing visibility and recognition for participants’ work. Who Should Apply? The programme welcomes: Young creators aged 18 to 29 Youth-led organizations Emerging digital creators Micro creators Advocacy content creators Youth educators Social media storytellers Young campaigners and communicators Importantly, follower count is NOT a selection criterion. Applicants do not need to be famous influencers or established creators to qualify. Priority will be given to applicants interested in: Comprehensive sexuality education Sexual and reproductive health and rights Gender equality Healthy relationships Ethical digital communication Positive masculinity Safe online engagement The programme also aims to ensure strong representation across: Geography Gender Disability inclusion Diverse youth communities across Asia and the Pacific Selection Criteria UNFPA is looking for applicants who demonstrate: Passion for positive digital impact Ethical and responsible online communication Interest in youth-focused advocacy Commitment to inclusive and safe content creation Willingness to learn and collaborate Certification Requirements To graduate from the programme and receive the UNFPA Young Creators Lab Completion Certificate, participants must: Attend sessions Pass all quizzes Complete sprint assignments Follow programme community guidelines The capstone project is optional and not mandatory for certification. How to Apply Application Deadline: 17 July 2026 Interested applicants can apply through the official application portal HERE . Click HERE for more information about the UNFPA Young Creators Lab 2026. Explore more global job opportunities on OFY here . The post UNFPA Young Creators Lab 2026: Fully Online Regional Programme for Young Digital Creators Across Asia and the Pacific appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Apply for the SheConnects Digital Accelerator Africa Round 2: Funding and Partnership Opportunity to Scale Women’s Digital Empowerment in Sub-Saharan Africa
summary: Organizations working to bridge the digital gender gap across Africa are invited to apply for Round 2 of the SheConnects Digital Accelerator: Africa , an initiative designed to support innovative partnerships that empower women and girls through digital technologies. Managed by CARE in partnership with the GSMA Foundation and supported by the Gates Foundation , the SheConnects Digital Accelerator aims to identify and scale proven, high-impact solutions that enable women and girls to fully participate in the digital economy. Round 2 officially opens 20 May 2026 and focuses on Partnerships for Women s Digital Empowerment at Scale , providing an opportunity for organizations to expand impactful digital initiatives while contributing to a more inclusive digital ecosystem across Sub-Saharan Africa. Application Status Applications are now open. Applicants are encouraged to review the official Request for Applications and submit their proposals through the official application process. About the SheConnects Digital Accelerator The SheConnects Digital Accelerator: Africa was established to address one of the continent s most pressing development challenges—the digital gender divide. Across many parts of Sub-Saharan Africa, women and girls continue to face barriers to accessing digital technologies, affordable internet, digital skills, and online economic opportunities. These challenges limit their participation in education, entrepreneurship, employment, financial inclusion, and broader social development. The Accelerator seeks to change this by supporting organizations with tested solutions that can be expanded to reach more women and girls. Rather than focusing solely on technology, the programme emphasizes partnerships, evidence generation, and scalable innovation capable of creating lasting systemic change. Round 2 Theme The second round of the Accelerator is centered on: Partnerships for Women s Digital Empowerment at Scale Successful applicants will be organizations or partnerships capable of delivering impactful digital solutions that improve women s participation in the digital world while generating evidence that can influence future policies and digital inclusion strategies. Programme Objectives The SheConnects Digital Accelerator seeks to: Increase meaningful digital adoption among women and girls. Improve access to the internet and digital services. Strengthen women s economic opportunities through technology. Support innovative partnerships across sectors. Generate evidence on effective digital inclusion strategies. Promote south-to-south knowledge sharing. Strengthen inclusive digital ecosystems across Africa. Influence future digital policies and programming. What the Accelerator Supports The programme is looking for proven solutions that can be expanded to create greater impact. Potential initiatives may focus on: Digital literacy and skills development. Women s access to affordable internet. Digital entrepreneurship. Financial inclusion through digital platforms. Digital livelihoods. Technology-enabled education. Women s online safety. Digital innovation. Community-based digital inclusion initiatives. Partnerships that improve access to digital opportunities. Projects should demonstrate clear evidence of impact and strong potential for scaling. Why Women s Digital Inclusion Matters Digital technologies have become essential for participating in modern economies and societies. When women have equitable access to digital tools and internet connectivity, they gain opportunities to: Access education. Start and grow businesses. Improve household incomes. Access healthcare information. Participate in financial services. Build professional networks. Strengthen resilience during economic shocks. Closing the digital gender gap also contributes to broader economic growth and sustainable development across Africa. Partnership Approach One of the distinguishing features of the Accelerator is its emphasis on collaboration. Organizations are encouraged to build partnerships that bring together: Civil society organizations. Technology companies. Government institutions. Research organizations. Innovation hubs. Community-based organizations. Private sector partners. These collaborations help ensure solutions are sustainable, scalable, and capable of influencing digital ecosystems. Round 1 Success During the first round, CARE partnered with four African organizations that are advancing women s meaningful use of digital technologies: Centre for Information Technology and Development KICTANet Smart Regional Consultants TechHer These organizations are implementing innovative initiatives designed to improve digital access, strengthen livelihoods, and generate valuable evidence that can shape future digital inclusion efforts across the continent. Programme Partners The Accelerator is delivered through a partnership between: CARE GSMA Foundation with financial support from: Gates Foundation Together, these organizations are working to accelerate digital inclusion and empower women across Sub-Saharan Africa through innovation, collaboration, and evidence-based programming. Benefits of Participating Organizations selected for the Accelerator can benefit from: Support to scale proven digital solutions. Opportunities to build strategic partnerships. Increased visibility for innovative initiatives. Access to learning and knowledge-sharing networks. Contribution to research and evidence generation. Opportunities to influence digital inclusion policies. Strengthened organizational capacity. Regional collaboration across Sub-Saharan Africa. Why You Should Apply If your organization is already delivering impactful digital solutions that empower women and girls, the SheConnects Digital Accelerator offers an opportunity to expand your reach, strengthen partnerships, and contribute to transforming digital inclusion across Africa. Through collaboration, innovation, and evidence-based learning, participating organizations can help shape a future where women and girls have equal opportunities to thrive in the digital economy. Interested organizations should review the official Request for Applications and submit their proposals through the official SheConnects Digital Accelerator application portal. Apply Here For more information on this grant opportunity visit here. title: Borealis Philanthropy Launches Just Futures Initiative Grant: Up to $200,000 Available for U.S. Grassroots Organizations Advancing Community Safety and Democracy
summary: Borealis Philanthropy has officially launched the Just Futures Initiative , a new grant opportunity designed to invest in grassroots organizations, coalitions, and collaborative formations working to advance community safety, democracy protection, civil rights, and belonging across the United States. The initiative builds on Borealis Philanthropy s longstanding commitment to supporting community-led solutions that reduce criminalization, strengthen democratic participation, and promote justice for communities most affected by policing, incarceration, surveillance, detention, and state violence. Unlike many traditional grant opportunities, the Just Futures Initiative provides flexible general operating support , allowing organizations to adapt their work to changing community needs throughout the grant period. Applications are accepted on a rolling basis while funding remains available. About Borealis Philanthropy Borealis Philanthropy is a movement-centered philanthropic intermediary dedicated to building an inclusive, multiracial democracy where everyone experiences dignity, safety, belonging, and justice. Through partnerships with grassroots organizations, movement leaders, and donors, Borealis invests in transformational strategies that strengthen communities and advance social justice. Its work spans several issue areas, including: Community Safety and Justice Democracy Protection Civil Rights Grassroots Leadership Social Justice Movements Community Organizing The Just Futures Initiative represents one of Borealis newest investments under its Community Safety Justice portfolio. About the Just Futures Initiative The Just Futures Initiative expands Borealis Philanthropy s long-standing investments in: Community safety Decarceration Protest defense Community accountability Alternatives to punishment Civic participation Democracy protection The initiative recognizes that the people closest to community challenges are also those best positioned to create lasting solutions. Rather than relying on punitive systems, the initiative seeks to strengthen community-led approaches rooted in care, protection, organizing, and collective power. Purpose of the Grant The Just Futures Initiative supports organizations building long-term community infrastructure that promotes: Community-defined safety Civil and human rights Democratic participation Freedom from criminalization Community belonging Collective resilience The initiative seeks to resource organizations already creating alternatives that reduce dependence on policing, incarceration, detention, and punishment. Priority Funding Areas Organizations applying should align their work with one of the initiative s three funding priorities. 1. Movement Defense and Protection of Civil and Human Rights This funding stream supports initiatives that protect communities experiencing heightened criminalization and state repression. Examples include: Protest defense Civil liberties advocacy Legal protection Community defense strategies Human rights initiatives Surveillance response 2. Community-Defined Safety and Alternatives to Criminalization This area funds community-driven approaches that create safer neighborhoods without relying on punitive systems. Examples include: Crisis response models Violence interruption Community accountability Restorative practices Local safety infrastructure Policy reform Alternatives to policing Alternatives to detention 3. Local Democracy Protection and Belonging Infrastructure This funding supports organizations strengthening civic participation and democratic inclusion. Examples include: Community organizing Civic engagement Democracy protection Local leadership development Community participation Inclusive governance Coalition building Grant Amounts The inaugural Just Futures Initiative offers 12-month general operating grants . Funding includes: Standard Grants USD $50,000–$100,000 Strategic Collaborative Grants Up to USD $200,000 Most successful applicants are expected to receive awards between: USD $50,000 and USD $75,000 Collaborative initiatives with strong ecosystem-building or rapid-response justifications may receive larger awards. As a guideline, grant requests should generally not exceed 30% of an organization s annual operating budget , unless justified by collaborative or emergency needs. Who Should Apply? The initiative welcomes proposals from: Grassroots nonprofits Community organizations Coalitions Collaborative partnerships Fiscally sponsored projects Applicants should demonstrate: Strong community trust Leadership rooted in lived experience Accountability to impacted communities Experience organizing around justice and community safety Clear strategies for long-term systems change Eligibility Requirements Organizations must: Be a U.S.-based or U.S. territory-based 501(c)(3) nonprofit or fiscally sponsored project. Have an annual operating budget generally below USD $2 million. Work directly with communities most affected by criminalization. Demonstrate expertise in community safety, organizing, or democracy protection. Center community leadership in their work. Include a clear organizing and power-building strategy. Priority consideration will be given to organizations serving underfunded Southern communities, rural areas, and high-threat regions. What Borealis Wants to Support Successful proposals should clearly explain: The community challenge being addressed. Why the issue is urgent. The proposed strategy. Activities planned during the 12-month grant period. Expected outcomes. Community relationships supporting the work. Leadership accountability. Organizational readiness. The initiative values flexibility and understands that community needs evolve. Grantees will have the freedom to adapt activities throughout the grant period while remaining aligned with their proposed goals. Learning and Collaboration Opportunities Beyond financial support, selected grantees may also participate in: Peer learning sessions Community of practice discussions Ecosystem-building opportunities Strategic collaboration Future initiative planning These engagements will help organizations share lessons, strengthen networks, and contribute to the future direction of the Just Futures Initiative. Application Process The application consists of two stages. Stage One: Letter of Interest (LOI) Eligible organizations begin by completing an online Letter of Interest after passing the eligibility quiz. The LOI asks applicants to explain: The issue or opportunity being addressed Their proposed strategy Expected outcomes Organizational readiness Collaborative partnerships (if applicable) Organizations selected during this stage will be invited to submit a full proposal. Stage Two: Full Proposal Invited applicants will submit: Detailed project narrative Organizational budget Grant budget Leadership information Optional supporting documents Letter of Interest Questions Applicants should prepare responses covering: The community challenge, threat, or opportunity. The proposed strategy over the 12-month grant period. Expected outcomes and long-term impact. Community relationships and leadership experience. Collaborative partners and coordination plans (if applicable). Application Timeline The Just Futures Initiative is currently accepting rolling applications . There is no fixed application deadline . Applications will be reviewed through multiple Letter of Interest cohorts, with funding decisions made on a rolling basis while grant funds remain available. Interested organizations are encouraged to apply as early as possible. How to Apply Organizations should begin by completing the Just Futures Eligibility Quiz . Eligible applicants will receive access to the online Letter of Interest application. Following review, selected organizations will be invited to submit a full proposal. Applicants are also encouraged to subscribe to the Borealis Philanthropy newsletter to receive updates about future funding opportunities. Apply Here For more information on this grant opportunity visit here Discover more grant opportunities on OFY, click here The post Borealis Philanthropy Launches Just Futures Initiative Grant: Up to $200,000 Available for U.S. Grassroots Organizations Advancing Community Safety and Democracy appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Wellcome Career Development Awards 2026 | Up to 8 Years of Funding for Mid-Career Researchers Worldwide
summary: The Wellcome Career Development Awards 2026 are now open for applications from outstanding mid-career researchers who are ready to become internationally recognized leaders in research. This prestigious funding programme provides long-term support for ambitious researchers from any discipline whose work has the potential to transform understanding of human life, health, and wellbeing. Designed to empower researchers at a critical stage of their careers, the award offers flexible funding for innovative research programmes, leadership development, and career progression over a period of up to eight years . Application Deadline: 28 July 2026 (15:00 BST) About the Wellcome Career Development Awards The Wellcome Career Development Awards support researchers who are prepared to lead substantial, creative, and high-impact research programmes that can generate major advances in scientific knowledge, methodologies, technologies, conceptual frameworks, or health-related research. Rather than prescribing a specific research theme, Wellcome encourages bold, curiosity-driven ideas from across multiple disciplines, provided they ultimately contribute to improving human life, health, or wellbeing. The programme aims to help successful applicants: Become internationally recognized research leaders. Build long-term independent research programmes. Develop leadership and management capabilities. Mentor the next generation of researchers. Foster positive and inclusive research environments. Produce research that drives significant advances in knowledge. Funding Overview Unlike many research grants that impose fixed budgets, Wellcome allows applicants to request the funding necessary to deliver their research programme. Funding Features Flexible funding based on research needs. Typical annual expenditure is below £250,000 (excluding the applicant s salary). Applications requesting larger budgets are permitted but will undergo additional scrutiny. Funding duration is generally 8 years , although some disciplines may receive shorter awards. Part-time awards are also available where appropriate. What Does the Award Cover? The Career Development Award provides comprehensive research support, including: Salary Support Where eligible, applicants may request funding for: Principal investigator salary Research staff salaries Research Costs Funding may also cover: Research personnel Laboratory materials Consumables Scientific equipment Animal research costs Clinical research expenses Data collection Fieldwork Travel Subsistence Overseas allowances Public engagement activities Patient involvement Open access publication charges Continuing professional development Leadership training Research management costs Institutional overheads (where applicable) Applicants are encouraged to develop budgets that realistically reflect the scope of their proposed research. Who Can Apply? The programme targets researchers who have moved beyond early-career stages and are ready to lead independent research. Applicants should already be: Leading their own research agenda. Making important contributions to their field. Building collaborative research partnerships. Beginning to supervise or mentor other researchers. Candidates are expected to demonstrate both scientific excellence and leadership potential. Career Stage Requirements Applicants should have completed: One or two substantial postdoctoral or equivalent research periods. They should also have: Significant research outputs. Experience driving independent research. Evidence of innovation. Leadership potential. Researchers may currently hold: Permanent appointments. Fixed-term contracts. Tenure-track positions. Eligibility varies depending on employment status and salary arrangements. Research Areas Supported One of the strengths of this programme is its openness to researchers across virtually every academic discipline. Eligible research areas include: Biomedical Sciences Public Health Clinical Research Experimental Medicine Genetics Genomics Neuroscience Behavioural Sciences Immunology Population Health Engineering Technology Data Science Artificial Intelligence Humanities Social Sciences Environmental Health Interdisciplinary Research The key requirement is that the proposed research has the potential to improve human life, health, or wellbeing. Research Assessment Criteria Applications undergo rigorous review based on three equally important dimensions. 1. Research Proposal (50%) Reviewers assess whether the research is: Innovative Ambitious Creative Well designed Evidence-based Capable of generating significant advances in understanding Strong proposals should demonstrate originality while remaining feasible. 2. Skills and Experience (25%) Applicants are evaluated based on: Previous research achievements. Scientific publications. Contributions to their discipline. Leadership experience. Mentoring record. Research management capability. 3. Research Environment (25%) Reviewers examine: Institutional support. Research culture. Leadership development opportunities. Commitment to diversity and inclusion. Strategic alignment between applicant and institution. Leadership Development A distinctive feature of the Career Development Award is its emphasis on developing future research leaders. Successful applicants are expected to: Lead multidisciplinary research teams. Build collaborative partnerships. Mentor junior researchers. Promote responsible research. Foster inclusive research cultures. Contribute to institutional research strategies. Global Talent Visa Endorsement Researchers awarded funding receive an important additional benefit. Successful award holders are automatically eligible for endorsement for a UK Global Talent Visa , making relocation and long-term research careers in the UK significantly easier. Eligible research team members may also qualify under the endorsed funder route. Application Process Applications are submitted through the Wellcome Funding Platform . Applicants should prepare: Complete online application form. Detailed research proposal. Budget justification. Institutional approvals. Letter of support (where required). Equipment quotations (if requesting equipment costing £100,000 or more). Applications must first be approved by the administering organization before submission to Wellcome. Selection Process The selection process consists of several stages: Stage 1: Eligibility Review Applications are checked for eligibility and alignment with programme objectives. Stage 2: Scientific Review Applications are evaluated by one of Wellcome s specialist Discovery Advisory Groups. Stage 3: External Peer Review Independent experts review the proposed research. Applicants receive reviewer comments before interview. Stage 4: Interview Shortlisted candidates attend an interview with the Career Development Award Interview Panel. Applicants deliver a presentation and answer questions regarding their research programme, leadership plans, and future vision. Stage 5: Final Funding Decision The Discovery Research Decision Board makes the final funding decisions. Key Dates Applications Open: 27 March 2026 Application Deadline: 28 July 2026 (15:00 BST) Shortlisting: October 2026 Interviews: 19–21 January 2027 Why Apply? The Wellcome Career Development Award is one of the world s leading mid-career research funding schemes. It offers far more than financial support—it provides a platform for researchers to establish global leadership in their field. Successful applicants benefit from: Up to eight years of flexible funding. Comprehensive research support. Leadership and professional development. International recognition. Opportunities to mentor future researchers. Funding across virtually all research disciplines. Global Talent Visa endorsement for UK-based awards. Freedom to pursue ambitious, curiosity-driven research. Researchers aiming to shape the future of science, health, technology, public policy, humanities, or interdisciplinary research are strongly encouraged to apply before the deadline. Application Deadline: 28 July 2026 (15:00 BST) Apply Here For more information on this grant opportunity visit here. title: UNFPA Communication Internship 2026 in Bangkok, Thailand | Fully Funded Learning Opportunity for Students and Recent Graduates
summary: The United Nations Population Fund (UNFPA) has opened applications for its Communication Internship at the Asia-Pacific Regional Office (APRO) in Bangkok, Thailand . This is an exciting opportunity for students and recent graduates who are passionate about communications, journalism, digital media, international development, and the work of the United Nations. The internship offers hands-on experience supporting one of the world s leading UN agencies in designing and implementing regional communication campaigns, managing digital platforms, creating editorial content, analyzing communication performance, and contributing to high-impact advocacy initiatives across Asia and the Pacific. Application Deadline: 11 July 2026 About UNFPA The United Nations Population Fund (UNFPA) is the UN s lead agency dedicated to ensuring: Every pregnancy is wanted. Every childbirth is safe. Every young person s potential is fulfilled. Through its 2026–2029 Strategic Plan , UNFPA is accelerating progress toward achieving the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) by focusing on four major outcomes: Ending the unmet need for family planning Ending preventable maternal deaths Ending gender-based violence and harmful practices Responding to demographic changes through evidence-based and rights-focused policies As part of this mission, UNFPA is looking for talented interns who are eager to contribute to meaningful global development initiatives while building valuable professional experience. Internship Location Duty Station: Bangkok, Thailand Office: UNFPA Asia-Pacific Regional Office (APRO) Work Schedule: Full-time Position Type: Internship Programme Grade: P1 What Will You Do? During the internship, you will work under the direct supervision of the Communications Specialist and the Regional Communications Adviser. Your responsibilities will include supporting communication activities across the Asia-Pacific region. Social Media Management You will assist in managing UNFPA APRO s official digital platforms, including: Facebook Instagram X (formerly Twitter) LinkedIn YouTube Responsibilities include: Preparing approved content Scheduling posts Monitoring engagement Supporting day-to-day platform management Content Creation Interns will help produce engaging communication materials such as: Web stories Human-interest features Blogs Articles Social media posts You will also assist with copy editing to ensure consistent messaging and high editorial quality. Communication Campaigns You will contribute to planning and coordinating regional communication campaigns that promote UNFPA s work across Asia and the Pacific. This includes supporting: Awareness campaigns Advocacy initiatives Regional communications projects Digital Analytics Interns will monitor communication performance across digital platforms by: Tracking engagement metrics Producing monthly analytics reports Measuring audience reach Supporting evidence-based communication planning Event Coordination The internship also includes logistical and administrative support for: Regional events Communication activities in Thailand Online webinars International meetings Campaign launches Content Planning You will collaborate with UNFPA country offices by: Collecting upcoming stories Updating editorial calendars Maintaining content planners Coordinating communication schedules Administrative Support Additional responsibilities may include: Conducting background research Organizing communication files Assisting with project coordination Supporting online and in-person communication activities Who Can Apply? Applicants must meet one of the following educational requirements: Be enrolled in a postgraduate degree programme; or Be a second-year or higher undergraduate student; or Be a recent university graduate who begins the internship within one year of graduation. Applicants should also: Demonstrate strong interest in international development Have excellent written and spoken English Be adaptable to multicultural working environments Build positive professional relationships with colleagues from diverse backgrounds Preferred Background UNFPA encourages applications from candidates with education in: Communications Journalism International Relations Public Relations Media Development Studies Related disciplines Candidates with volunteer or internship experience in: United Nations agencies NGOs Development organizations Community initiatives will have an added advantage. Skills and Competencies Successful applicants should possess: Excellent writing skills Strong analytical abilities Research experience Editing skills Social media management knowledge Familiarity with digital communication tools Good organizational skills Initiative and sound judgement Ability to work effectively in multicultural teams Learning Opportunities This internship provides extensive professional development through practical experience within a UN regional office. Participants will gain experience in: UN communications strategies Regional advocacy campaigns International development programming Social media management Editorial content production Knowledge management Partnership development Project coordination Cross-cultural collaboration Interns will also have opportunities to build professional networks with: UN colleagues Country offices Development partners Regional communication professionals Financial Support Although this internship is unpaid, eligible interns receive financial assistance. UNFPA Provides A stipend to help cover basic daily living expenses (for interns not already receiving financial support from another institution) Interns Must Cover Accommodation Travel Visa expenses (where applicable) Living expenses beyond the stipend Medical insurance Applicants must have valid medical insurance before beginning the internship. Diversity and Inclusion UNFPA is committed to creating an inclusive workplace and strongly encourages applications from: Women Persons with disabilities Indigenous peoples LGBTQIA+ individuals Minority ethnic communities Other underrepresented groups Reasonable accommodation is available for applicants with disabilities during the recruitment process. Why Apply? This internship offers an outstanding opportunity to: Gain professional experience with a United Nations agency Build expertise in international communications Work on regional campaigns with global impact Develop digital communication and social media skills Learn about international development and humanitarian communication Expand your professional network across Asia-Pacific and the UN system Strengthen your CV for future UN and international organization careers Apply Here For more information on this internship opportunity, visit here For more opportunities on OFY, visit here The post UNFPA Communication Internship 2026 in Bangkok, Thailand | Fully Funded Learning Opportunity for Students and Recent Graduates appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Teach For All Media and Editorial Coordinator (Remote): Global Communications Job Opportunity Across 35+ Countries
summary: Teach For All is inviting applications for the position of Media and Editorial Coordinator , offering an exciting opportunity for communications and media professionals who are passionate about storytelling, global education, and social impact. This fully remote position is open to applicants who are legally authorized to work in more than 35 countries across Africa, Asia, Europe, North America, South America, and Oceania. As Teach For All continues advancing its 25-year vision of ensuring that every child has access to quality education and the opportunity to reach their full potential, the organization is seeking a highly organized and creative professional to help amplify the voices, stories, and insights emerging from its global network. This role is ideal for professionals with experience in journalism, media relations, communications, editorial management, or public engagement who want to contribute to meaningful global change while working in an international, mission-driven environment. Application Status: Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis , so interested candidates are encouraged to apply as early as possible. About Teach For All Teach For All is a global network of more than 60 independent, locally led organizations working together to expand educational opportunity worldwide. The organization partners with local education leaders, teachers, governments, and communities to develop collective leadership that enables children from all backgrounds to thrive. Rather than operating schools directly, Teach For All supports its network partners through global learning, coaching, consulting, collaboration, and access to international resources that strengthen education systems around the world. About the Media and Public Engagement Team The Media and Public Engagement team helps shape global conversations around education by positioning Teach For All as a leading voice in educational equity and systems change. The team works proactively to: Build compelling narratives around Teach For All s mission. Share impactful stories from network partners. Engage global media outlets and journalists. Publish thought leadership content. Increase public awareness of education challenges and solutions. Support strategic communications across the organization s global network. The Media and Editorial Coordinator plays a central role in ensuring these efforts run efficiently while maintaining high editorial standards. Position Summary The Media and Editorial Coordinator will support media relations, editorial planning, thought leadership, and operational systems that enable Teach For All to communicate effectively with global audiences. The role combines strategic communication with hands-on project management, making it suitable for professionals who enjoy balancing creativity with operational excellence. Key Responsibilities 1. Media Relations (30%) Successful candidates will help strengthen Teach For All s relationships with journalists and media organizations by: Supporting media engagement activities. Managing relationships with journalists and editors. Conducting media landscape research. Identifying relevant media platforms for outreach. Maintaining media contact databases. Managing incoming media inquiries. Monitoring daily media coverage. Tracking Teach For All s media presence. Identifying new engagement opportunities. Analyzing media coverage and producing actionable insights. 2. Thought Leadership and Public Engagement (25%) The successful candidate will contribute to developing high-quality thought leadership content by: Supporting the creation of opinion articles (op-eds). Assisting with essays and expert commentary. Conducting background research. Drafting and editing written materials. Coordinating editorial submissions. Managing editorial calendars and publication workflows. Maintaining consistent editorial quality across content. Supporting written and video public engagement initiatives. Coordinating speaking opportunities. Preparing press releases and media kits. 3. Operations and Systems (45%) Operational excellence is a major component of this position. Responsibilities include: Managing spokesperson databases. Coordinating spokesperson preparation. Maintaining journalist CRM systems. Managing media monitoring systems. Tracking editorial output. Monitoring media placements. Maintaining workflow trackers. Supporting collaboration across internal teams. Coordinating event logistics. Scheduling meetings. Preparing briefing documents. Managing the team s media inquiry inbox. Supporting post-event follow-up activities. Who Should Apply? This opportunity is ideal for professionals working in: Journalism Media Relations Communications Public Relations Editorial Management Content Strategy Corporate Communications International Development Education Non-profit Communications Social Impact Organizations Candidates who enjoy storytelling, strategic communication, and global collaboration will be particularly well suited for this role. Required Skills and Qualifications Teach For All is looking for candidates with: Excellent writing and storytelling skills. Strong editing and proofreading abilities. A solid understanding of media relations. Knowledge of how newsrooms operate. Strong organizational and project management skills. Excellent communication and interpersonal skills. Ability to manage multiple priorities simultaneously. Analytical skills for media monitoring and reporting. Ability to work across cultures and time zones. A proactive, solutions-oriented mindset. Commitment to diversity, inclusion, and continuous learning. Preferred Experience While not mandatory, candidates with the following experience will be highly competitive: Three or more years of professional experience in journalism, communications, media relations, think tanks, or related fields. Experience working within education, international development, or social impact organizations. Familiarity with media monitoring platforms. Experience using Salesforce or similar CRM systems. Experience supporting spokesperson preparation or media training. What Teach For All Offers Successful candidates will benefit from: Fully remote work. A competitive salary based on country of employment and experience. Comprehensive employee benefits. Flexible working arrangements. Generous paid time off. Professional growth opportunities. International collaboration. Meaningful work with global impact. Opportunities for long-term career progression beyond the initial one-year contract. Employment Terms Successful candidates should note: Initial contract duration is one year . Strong potential for extension and career growth. Up to 20% travel may be required. Some meetings will occur outside traditional working hours due to global time zones. Visa sponsorship is not available . Applicants must already possess legal work authorization in their country of hire. Eligible Countries Applications are accepted from candidates legally authorized to work in the following locations: Australia Bangladesh Belgium Canada Chile Colombia Côte d Ivoire Dominican Republic Ecuador Ghana Hong Kong India Indonesia Italy Jordan Kenya Malawi Malaysia Mauritius Mexico Morocco New Zealand Nigeria Pakistan Peru Philippines Puerto Rico Qatar Rwanda Singapore South Africa Spain Thailand Uganda Zambia How to Apply Applicants should prepare: An up-to-date Resume or Curriculum Vitae (CV). A one-page motivation letter written in English explaining: Why they are interested in the role. How their experience aligns with the position. What they hope to contribute to Teach For All s mission. Click here for more and to apply Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis , so early submission is strongly encouraged. Why This Opportunity Stands Out For communications professionals seeking a globally impactful career, this role offers the rare opportunity to influence international conversations around education while collaborating with experts and organizations across more than 60 countries. Whether your passion lies in journalism, strategic communications, editorial excellence, or public engagement, joining Teach For All means becoming part of a mission-driven organization working to expand educational opportunity for children around the world. Click here for more global fellowship opportunities The post Teach For All Media and Editorial Coordinator (Remote): Global Communications Job Opportunity Across 35+ Countries appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Greenpeace MENA Remote Engagement Director Job Opportunity (2026): Strategic Leadership Role in Environmental Advocacy Across the MENA Region
summary: The environmental advocacy space continues to expand rapidly, and organizations are increasingly seeking experienced leaders who can drive meaningful engagement, strengthen supporter communities, and build powerful communication strategies. One such opportunity is the Remote Engagement Director position at Greenpeace MENA , a senior leadership role designed for professionals passionate about environmental change, strategic communication, and audience mobilization. This position plays a critical role in shaping how Greenpeace connects with supporters, builds campaigns, and strengthens its influence across the Middle East and North Africa (MENA) region. The application deadline for this opportunity is 23 July 2026 . Overview of the Engagement Director Role The Engagement Director is a senior-level leadership position within the GP MENA Engagement Department. The role reports directly to the Executive Director and operates as part of the Senior Management Team. This position is responsible for designing, managing, and refining engagement strategies that support Greenpeace’s mission, including communication, digital outreach, supporter engagement, and fundraising integration. The role is fully remote but focused on the MENA region, requiring strong cross-cultural leadership and collaboration across distributed teams. Key Responsibilities 1. Strategic Direction and Program Leadership The Engagement Director is responsible for shaping the overall engagement strategy, ensuring alignment with Greenpeace MENA’s mission and long-term goals. Key duties include: Designing integrated engagement strategies combining: Communications Digital campaigns Media outreach Community engagement initiatives Defining measurable KPIs for campaigns and fundraising success Leading strategic storytelling efforts to strengthen Greenpeace’s brand narrative Developing audience growth strategies to expand supporter bases across MENA Building strong media and stakeholder relationships across traditional and digital platforms Staying updated with trends in digital communications, advocacy tools, and NGO innovations Overseeing engagement budgets, resource allocation, and financial planning Aligning engagement strategies with fundraising goals for maximum impact 2. Leadership, Team Management, and Coaching This role includes direct responsibility for leading and developing the engagement team. Responsibilities include: Managing staff performance and setting clear objectives Providing coaching, mentoring, and professional development support Ensuring high-quality outputs through performance monitoring and feedback Coordinating with Programme and Fundraising Directors to maximize campaign impact Acting as a key representative for engagement across Greenpeace International networks Supporting global collaboration and shared campaign strategies 3. Audience Development and Supporter Engagement A major focus of the role is building strong, long-term relationships with supporters. Key responsibilities include: Designing and optimizing supporter journeys for new and existing audiences Enhancing supporter retention and loyalty through targeted engagement strategies Collaborating with fundraising teams to strengthen donor relationships Managing data systems for audience segmentation and performance tracking Improving supporter care through effective communication strategies Ensuring storytelling is embedded in all engagement campaigns 4. Data, Strategy, and Performance Management The role requires strong analytical and strategic planning capabilities. Key expectations include: Using data-driven insights to guide engagement decisions Developing forecasting models for income and engagement performance Managing reporting systems for internal and external stakeholders Improving digital engagement platforms and processes Ensuring effective measurement of campaign impact 5. External Partnerships and Network Building The Engagement Director will also: Build relationships with external partners, agencies, and suppliers Strengthen collaborations across media and advocacy networks Represent Greenpeace MENA in global Greenpeace initiatives Develop partnerships that amplify engagement reach and effectiveness Required Qualifications and Experience Educational Background Bachelor’s degree in: Marketing Communications Journalism Advertising Media Studies, or a related field Professional Experience Minimum of 10+ years of experience in engagement, communications, or advocacy roles Senior-level experience in NGO or international organization environments Proven leadership of teams and large-scale engagement programs Strong background in digital and offline audience mobilization Core Skills Expertise in engagement strategy and supporter acquisition Strong digital communications and marketing experience Proven data analysis and reporting capabilities Experience in fundraising or donor engagement strategies Ability to design supporter journeys and segmentation models Technical and Functional Competencies Applicants should demonstrate: Excellent English communication skills (Arabic is an advantage) Strong leadership and team development abilities Deep understanding of NGO operations and advocacy environments Ability to manage budgets and forecast performance Experience with media strategy and public engagement campaigns Strong interpersonal and stakeholder management skills Ability to work effectively in multicultural and remote teams Leadership Expectations The ideal candidate should be able to: Develop and execute long-term engagement strategies Lead cross-functional teams across multiple regions Act as a strategic advisor in campaign planning Empower teams and foster innovation in communication approaches Strengthen audience-centered organizational culture Influence internal and external stakeholders effectively Commitment and Organizational Values Greenpeace MENA operates under the broader mission of Greenpeace International, promoting environmental protection through non-violent advocacy and direct action campaigns. The organization is committed to: Environmental justice Peaceful activism Diversity and inclusion in hiring practices Equal opportunity employment regardless of background Application Process Interested candidates are required to submit: Updated CV Cover Letter Click here for more and to apply Deadline: 23 July 2026 Conclusion This Engagement Director role represents a high-impact leadership opportunity for professionals who are passionate about environmental advocacy, audience engagement, and strategic communication. It is a chance to shape regional campaigns, influence global conversations, and drive meaningful environmental action across the MENA region. title: Principles of Intelligence Bridging Barriers in Safety and Security Fellowship (PIBBSS) Winter Fellowship 2026–2027 in South Africa: Receive $3,000 Monthly Stipend, Free Accommodation, Flights, Mentorship, and Global Artificial Intelligence Safety Research Opportunities
summary: Applications are now open for the highly competitive Principles of Intelligence Bridging Barriers in Safety and Security (PIBBSS) Winter Fellowship 2026–2027 , an interdisciplinary Artificial Intelligence (AI) safety research fellowship designed for outstanding researchers from around the world. Hosted by Principles of Intelligence, the fellowship offers researchers a unique opportunity to transition into the rapidly growing field of AI safety while working alongside leading mentors, scholars, and practitioners. The program is specifically designed to connect talented researchers from diverse academic backgrounds with experienced AI safety mentors and research communities. The fellowship will run for approximately three months, from November 2026 to February 2027 , primarily in Cape Town, South Africa , with remote participation available only for exceptional candidates. Participants will receive generous financial support, dedicated mentorship, accommodation, travel support, and access to a vibrant interdisciplinary community focused on addressing some of the world s most important AI-related challenges. About the PIBBSS Fellowship The Principles of Intelligence Bridging Barriers in Safety and Security Fellowship is an intensive research program focused on advancing AI safety through interdisciplinary collaboration. The program recognizes that many of the most important questions surrounding advanced AI systems cannot be solved by computer science alone. Instead, meaningful progress requires contributions from researchers across numerous disciplines that study intelligence, complex systems, governance, ethics, cognition, and human behavior. The fellowship therefore brings together researchers from different academic and professional backgrounds and supports them in developing impactful AI safety research projects under expert guidance. Over the years, fellowship alumni have gone on to work in: AI safety laboratories Government institutions Universities Independent research organizations AI governance initiatives Technology policy organizations Past fellows have secured positions at leading institutions including Harvard University, the University of Oxford, Anthropic, Google, Epoch, and various AI safety organizations. Fellowship Duration and Location Successful applicants will participate in: A pre-program AI risk reading group conducted remotely A multi-day opening retreat Three months of full-time mentored research Community events and speaker sessions A final symposium presentation in Spring 2027 A closing retreat with fellow researchers and mentors Program Timeline Fellowship Period: November 2026 – February 2027 Duration: Approximately 3 months Location: Cape Town, South Africa Participation Mode: In-person (remote participation only for exceptional candidates) Benefits of the PIBBSS Winter Fellowship 2026–2027 The fellowship provides substantial support to ensure participants can fully focus on their research. Financial Benefits Selected fellows will receive: Monthly stipend of $3,000 Funding for approximately three months One return flight to Cape Town, South Africa Visa support letters where required Accommodation and Living Support Participants will also receive: Free accommodation Private bedroom for each fellow Shared apartment arrangements where applicable Daily workday meals Lunch, dinner, and snacks provided at the office Academic and Professional Benefits Fellows will gain access to: Dedicated AI safety mentors Personalized project development support Research strategy coaching Interdisciplinary collaboration opportunities Regular speaker talks Professional networking events Shared office space Final symposium presentation opportunities Career Development Benefits The fellowship is particularly valuable for researchers seeking to: Transition into AI safety research Build international research networks Develop publishable research outputs Engage with leading AI safety experts Explore AI governance and policy pathways Access future career opportunities in academia, industry, and public policy Research Areas and Fields Encouraged to Apply The fellowship actively seeks researchers from diverse disciplines that contribute insights into intelligence, decision-making, governance, and complex systems. Relevant fields include: Mathematics Neuroscience Cognitive Science Dynamical Systems Theory Physics Philosophy Political Theory Economic Theory Ecology Evolutionary Biology Linguistics Media Studies Humanities Related research disciplines Researchers from interdisciplinary backgrounds are strongly encouraged to apply. Who Can Apply? The fellowship is intended for individuals who are motivated to contribute to AI safety and possess research experience in fields related to complex and intelligent systems. Applicants may include: PhD researchers Doctoral candidates Postdoctoral researchers Academic researchers Independent researchers Experienced professionals with significant research backgrounds Importantly, applicants do not need to hold a specific academic credential if they can demonstrate substantial research experience. The fellowship welcomes applicants from all countries worldwide. Another major advantage is that applicants are not required to have a fully developed research project before applying. The fellowship team works closely with selected candidates during the interview process to identify suitable mentors and refine project ideas. Fellowship Structure The fellowship follows a carefully designed progression that supports fellows throughout the research journey. Pre-Program Phase Participants begin with: Remote reading groups AI risk discussions Foundational AI safety materials Community-building activities Research Phase During the core fellowship period, fellows engage in: Full-time research Regular mentorship meetings Research coaching sessions Collaborative discussions Community events Final Presentation Phase The program concludes with: Research presentations Symposium participation Networking opportunities Knowledge-sharing sessions Application Process The selection process is conducted in multiple stages. Stage 1: Written Application Applicants must submit: Curriculum Vitae (CV) or résumé Personal statement (600–800 words) Research background information Motivation for pursuing AI safety research Optional but recommended work samples Stages 2–4: Interviews and Project Development Shortlisted candidates will participate in: Multiple interview rounds Research interest discussions Project proposal development Mentor matching sessions The selection process is designed to identify talented researchers while ensuring strong alignment between fellows and mentors. Why the PIBBSS Fellowship Stands Out Unlike many traditional research fellowships, the PIBBSS Fellowship emphasizes interdisciplinary collaboration and mentorship. Participants join a global network of researchers who are exploring: AI alignment AI governance AI ethics Cognitive science and AI intersections Policy responses to emerging AI risks Human and machine intelligence comparisons Long-term AI safety challenges This collaborative environment enables fellows to contribute fresh perspectives and innovative approaches to some of the most pressing issues in artificial intelligence. Application Deadline The deadline to apply for the PIBBSS Winter Fellowship 2026–2027 is: 20 July 2026 (23:59 Anywhere on Earth). Applicants are encouraged to submit their applications as early as possible to allow sufficient time for review and interview scheduling. Researchers interested in advancing AI safety while working alongside world-class mentors in an international environment should strongly consider applying for this fully funded fellowship opportunity. APPLY HERE For more information about this opportunity, visit here . Discover more Global Fellowship Opportunities on OFY here . title: Canada’s Mexico Fintech Canadian Technology Accelerator 2026: Expand Your Fintech Business into Latin America’s Fast-Growing Market
summary: Canadian fintech companies looking to expand internationally now have an exceptional opportunity to enter one of Latin America s fastest-growing digital finance markets through the Mexico Fintech Canadian Technology Accelerator (CTA) 2026 . Delivered by the Trade Commissioner Service (TCS) under the Government of Canada s Canadian Technology Accelerators (CTA) initiative, this 13-week hybrid business development program is designed to help innovative Canadian fintech companies successfully explore, enter, and grow within Mexico s rapidly expanding financial technology ecosystem. With tailored mentorship, business matchmaking, investor introductions, and an immersive in-market experience in Mexico City, the program equips participants with the knowledge, partnerships, and market intelligence needed for successful international expansion. Application Deadline 15 July 2026 About the Mexico Fintech Canadian Technology Accelerator Mexico has become one of the most attractive fintech destinations in Latin America. Several factors have contributed to this momentum, including: Rapid growth of the fintech industry A large underbanked and underserved population Progressive fintech regulations Increasing digital financial adoption Strong investor interest Growing demand for innovative financial technologies Mexico was the first country in Latin America to introduce a comprehensive fintech law, creating an environment that encourages innovation while welcoming foreign fintech companies. Today, Mexico is the second-largest fintech ecosystem in Latin America , growing at approximately 20% annually and projected to surpass 86 million digital financial service users by the end of 2027 . These market dynamics create tremendous opportunities for Canadian fintech innovators specializing in: Digital payments Credit solutions Financial infrastructure Enterprise fintech Financial inclusion Cybersecurity Emerging financial technologies The Canadian Technology Accelerator helps participating companies capitalize on these opportunities through structured business development support. Program Dates Program Duration: 26 August – 20 November 2026 Application Deadline: 15 July 2026 Weekly Commitment: Approximately 2.5 hours The accelerator follows a hybrid delivery model that combines virtual programming with an in-person business development mission to Mexico City. Why Join the Mexico Fintech CTA? Participating companies receive practical support designed specifically for international business expansion. The accelerator goes beyond traditional training by facilitating direct engagement with potential customers, investors, regulators, and strategic partners. Program Benefits Selected companies will gain access to numerous advantages, including: Comprehensive Market Intelligence Participants receive an in-depth briefing covering: Mexican fintech regulations Business culture Market opportunities Import considerations Commercial best practices Market entry strategies Targeted Business-to-Business Meetings Companies will participate in carefully curated meetings with: Government officials Industry experts Venture capital firms Business chambers Financial institutions Regulators Potential clients Potential distributors Strategic partners Personalized Mentorship Every company benefits from tailored mentoring designed to help them: Refine market entry strategies Improve investor pitches Strengthen business positioning Develop partnerships Navigate Mexico s fintech ecosystem Expert Workshops Participants will join workshops covering topics such as: Doing business in Mexico Mexican fintech regulations Investment opportunities Industry trends Market expansion strategies Investor and Buyer Exposure The accelerator creates opportunities to meet: Investors Venture capital firms Potential buyers Corporate partners Strategic collaborators Pitching Opportunities Throughout the program, companies will have multiple opportunities to present their innovations directly to key decision-makers in Mexico. Who Should Apply? The accelerator seeks innovative Canadian fintech companies offering differentiated solutions in areas including: Digital payments Cross-border payment technologies Alternative lending Financial inclusion Fraud prevention Cybersecurity Payroll platforms Human resource technologies Employee compensation platforms Eligibility Requirements To qualify, applicants must demonstrate the following: Validated Technology Your company should offer an innovative and differentiated product, technology, or service. Proven Market Fit Applicants should have: An established customer base Product-market validation Clearly identified target users Business Traction Evidence of company maturity may include: Revenue generation Investment received A minimum viable product (MVP) Customer adoption Other indicators of commercial success Strong Leadership Companies should have an experienced management team with at least one senior executive able to participate throughout the entire accelerator. Capacity for International Expansion Applicants must possess sufficient financial and organizational resources to successfully pursue growth in Mexico. Program Structure The 13-week accelerator consists of four phases. Phase 1: Market Preparedness 26 August – 25 September 2026 Activities include: Individual introductory meetings Onboarding session Webinar: How to Do Business in Mexico Webinar: Mexican Fintech Ecosystem Initial mentoring sessions Phase 2: Business Development and Matchmaking 28 September – 6 November 2026 During this phase, companies receive: Tailored B2B meetings Business matchmaking Client introductions Distributor introductions Ongoing mentorship Phase 3: In-Market Business Mission Mexico City | 9–13 November 2026 Participants travel to Mexico City for an intensive week of business engagement featuring: Embassy plenary sessions Curated B2B meetings Follow-up meetings Site visits Expert roundtables Networking receptions Pitch sessions Cohort activities Final mentoring sessions Phase 4: Post-Program Support 16–20 November 2026 The accelerator concludes with: Follow-up meetings Remaining mentorship sessions Offboarding session Business continuity planning Why Mexico? Mexico continues to emerge as one of the world s most promising fintech markets. Its rapidly expanding digital economy, supportive regulatory framework, increasing smartphone adoption, and growing demand for financial innovation make it an ideal destination for Canadian fintech companies seeking international expansion. The country offers opportunities across virtually every fintech segment while providing direct access to Latin America s broader financial ecosystem. Why Participate? The Mexico Fintech Canadian Technology Accelerator provides much more than business training. Participants receive personalized support from experienced trade professionals while building relationships with investors, customers, regulators, and strategic partners that can accelerate international growth. For Canadian fintech companies ready to scale beyond domestic markets, this accelerator represents an outstanding opportunity to establish a lasting presence in one of Latin America s most dynamic financial technology ecosystems.
